summary: Implemented sensitive content sanitizer in internal/sanitizer/sanitizer.go with Sanitize(content string) (string, bool). Uses regexp.MustCompile for pattern (?i)(api_key|secret|token|password)([\s:=]+)(['"]?)(\S+). Replaces captured value with *** while preserving key name and separator. Handles CJK content without false positives.
keyDecisions: Captured separator and optional quote as separate regex groups to preserve them in output (key=*** vs key=*** vs key:*** vs key ***)
Removed unreachable nil-check on FindStringSubmatch result since ReplaceAllStringFunc only calls for actual matches, achieving 100% coverage
Used ReplaceAllStringFunc + FindStringSubmatch instead of ReplaceAllString with $N replacements to avoid losing separator/quote context

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Pattern: (?i)(api_key|secret|token|password)[\s:=]+["']?(\S+) matches and replaces captured value
Sanitized string does NOT contain the original sensitive value
Handles CJK content correctly (no false positives on Chinese characters)
Unit tests: pattern matching, false positives, CJK content, edge cases

notes: Coverage is 100% (exceeds 95% requirement). 20 test cases cover: pattern matching for all 4 keywords, case insensitivity, no-match, empty string, multiple matches, CJK/Chinese/Japanese content (no false positives), quoted values (single and double), space/colon/equals separators, key name preservation, and mixed language content.
